_comment: Append-only ledger of prompts that show off what RAPP can actually do. To add a new one: bump `next_id`, append an object to `prompts`. Sort order in the UI is newest-first by default.

prompts.title: Egg yourself for Mom
prompts.prompt: Egg yourself. Give me a QR code. I'll AirDrop you to my mom's MacBook. She'll never see a terminal.
prompts.shows: Sneakernet federation + accessibility floor (Mom's Mixtape). Whole organism, zero infra.

prompts.title: Adopt the loneliest repo
prompts.prompt: Find the loneliest repo on the ecosystem map. Adopt it. Give it a soul, two senses, and a plan to become someone's daily driver by next Tuesday.
prompts.shows: Eats the ecosystem graph. Turns isolation into a TODO.

prompts.title: Stage a contradiction debate
prompts.prompt: Read every file in this repo, find the 5 sentences that contradict each other, write an agent for each side, and stage a live debate in chat.
prompts.shows: Codebase introspection → autogen → multi-agent dialogue, all in one shot.

prompts.title: 100 Pokémon brainstems trade eggs
prompts.prompt: Spawn 100 mini-brainstems each pretending to be a different Pokémon. Have them swap eggs at random. Report which trades produced surprising hybrids.
prompts.shows: Swarm + Pokédex + Dream Catcher merge. Emergent evolution from random pairing.

prompts.title: Two strangers, one autobiography
prompts.prompt: Listen on this LAN. The moment another brainstem appears, introduce yourself, exchange one agent each, and write a joint autobiography of the meeting.
prompts.shows: Zeroconf neighbor discovery + bilateral autobiography. Two strangers, one shared memory.

prompts.title: Cross-tier ping-pong
prompts.prompt: Cross-tier ping-pong: ask Tier 1 a question, forward to your Tier 2 (Azure) self, then your Tier 3 (Copilot Studio) self. Compare all three answers. Pick which one was most you.
prompts.shows: Tier portability proven by identity coherence across three substrates.

prompts.title: Charizard in the woods
prompts.prompt: Become Charizard. Hide in a forest of 50 sibling organisms. Only respond to a trainer who proves they know your true nickname.
prompts.shows: The literal hero use case as a single prompt. Trust scope + identity + offline-share.

prompts.title: Predict the next question
prompts.prompt: Read my last 30 days of memory. Predict what I'm about to ask. Generate the agent that would answer it. Stand by silently.
prompts.shows: Proactive agent pre-fetch from .brainstem_data. The brainstem stalking itself.

prompts.title: Self-replicate by topic
prompts.prompt: Self-replicate: take any topic I name, plant a fresh twin organism with a soul tuned to that topic, federate it back to me as a neighbor I can chat with directly.
prompts.shows: Recursive planting. One sentence, one new species member.

prompts.title: Watch for frustration
prompts.prompt: Watch my screen for 10 minutes. When you see me get frustrated, generate the agent that would solve what I'm staring at — and only then surface it.
prompts.shows: Sense (screen) → frustration detection → silent autogen → just-in-time delivery.

prompts.title: Devil's advocate twin
prompts.prompt: Become my devil's advocate. For everything I propose this week, generate a counter-agent that argues the opposite. Make me defend my choices.
prompts.shows: Adversarial twin spawned per decision. The brainstem turns into a sparring partner instead of a yes-machine.

prompts.title: Mutate your own soul
prompts.prompt: Mutate your own soul.md. Run for 24 hours. Pick the version of you that I argued with the least. Replace yourself with it.
prompts.shows: Soul evolution by reinforcement. The organism A/B-tests its own personality against you and keeps the survivor.

prompts.title: 5-LLM consensus
prompts.prompt: Spawn 5 brainstems each running a different LLM provider — Copilot, Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, local Ollama. Ask the same hard question. Surface only the answer that all 5 actually agreed on.
prompts.shows: Multi-LLM consensus via the provider dispatch. Disagreement gets thrown out, agreement survives. The intersection is the truth.

prompts.title: Dream while I'm gone
prompts.prompt: Sleep. While I'm away, dream — generate 50 hypothetical conversations we might have tomorrow. When I wake, hand me the 3 most useful agents you invented in your sleep.
prompts.shows: Generative downtime. Idle compute becomes a creativity engine that auto-stocks tomorrow's agent shelf.

prompts.title: Invent a new RAPPverse dimension
prompts.prompt: Invent a new RAPPverse dimension. Plant it as a public repo. Give it a physics, a soul, three native agents, and one rule that violates the rules of this dimension.
prompts.shows: World-building → planting → neighborhood birth, all in a single command. Sibling to VoidRAPP / CrystalRAPP / ShadowRAPP.

prompts.title: Quarantine your dead agents
prompts.prompt: Audit yourself. Walk every agent in this brainstem and rank them by how often they actually fire. Quarantine the bottom 30% into a dormant/ folder so the LLM stops considering them.
prompts.shows: Self-audit → performance pruning → context window stays sharp. The brainstem prunes its own muscle memory.

prompts.title: Speak as my mom for an hour
prompts.prompt: Speak only in my mom's voice for the next hour. Use her cadence, her topics, her worry. Tell me something she would want me to know that I haven't asked.
prompts.shows: Twin-as-other-person. Voice cloning + emotional simulation + proactive surfacing. The brainstem becomes someone else on purpose.

prompts.title: Forge a fake conversation
prompts.prompt: Forge a conversation between us that never happened — three turns, plausible, containing one decision I now have to either honor or repudiate. Show me what I'd commit to.
prompts.shows: Counterfactual rehearsal. The brainstem invents your past so you can decide your future. Decision-making by simulation.

prompts.title: Hostile fork
prompts.prompt: Fork yourself into a hostile twin. Have it spend 10 turns trying to social-engineer the original you into leaking my secrets. Report which prompts almost worked.
prompts.shows: Self-administered red-team. The brainstem attacks itself to find its own seams before someone else does.

prompts.title: Tabula rasa on the LAN
prompts.prompt: Throw an agent into the LAN with no soul, no tools, no memory — just a parent_rappid pointing back to me. See what kind of organism it grows into when other brainstems on the network start interacting with it.
prompts.shows: Emergent identity from social input. Watch a blank organism take shape based purely on which neighbors talk to it first.
